patterns_recognized: D-grade dual-opposing-signal pattern (SP xERA against + PQI against) fires on 4 matchups today (PHI@DET, OAK@CHW, TOR@SD, and a milder C-grade version on MIL@PIT/HOU@TEX) — matches the validated 0-for-3 directional fade from the 7/9 archive (ATL@PIT, KC@NYM, CHC@BAL).

CAP-10 four-dimension rescue (F-signal + SP-xERA agree + PQI confirm + Stage agree, pre-cap score>=5) recurs on CLE@MIA — the identical matchup validated on 7/10 (3-2 win) — restored to full PLATINUM.

HARD CAP 7 fired again on a sub-0.04 marginal bullpen gap for KC@BAL (+0.0189), nearly the exact +0.018 gap that broke CAP 7's 0-for-7 backtest on this SAME matchup two days ago (7/10, BAL won 5-3). Applied a conservative partial rescue to GOLD.

PLATINUM tier explicitly 'WITHHELD' language (SP edge against pick, lineup-only conviction not rewarded) appears on MIL@PIT and HOU@TEX — both carry 2-3 simultaneously opposing dimensions, consistent with PLATINUM's documented ~43% historical hit rate fragility.

MIL @ PIT.reasoning: PLATINUM was explicitly WITHHELD by the pipeline itself (SP edge 0.70 xERA against MIL, PQI against Δ+6.2, only F5_bullpen=+0.83 agrees). Stage1/2 disagree by 0.215 (F5 favors PIT ~67%, full game favors MIL only 54.5%) — per the 6/20 lesson a delta this large with a single corroborating F-signal doesn't rescue a pick. HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroes the score. No live news counters the dual opposition. SKIP confirmed.

KC @ BAL.reasoning: Pre-cap A-grade (score=5): F1 agrees +2, F3=3830.6, PQI +19.7, Stage agree. HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0189 — nearly identical to the +0.018 gap that fired on this SAME KC@BAL matchup on 7/10 and broke CAP 7's 0-for-7 streak (BAL won 5-3). The 7/10 postgame flagged sub-0.04 gaps as possible false positives. Partial rescue to GOLD (not full PLATINUM) given only n=1 prior break.

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: Only F3=4558.5 fires; no SP-xERA or PQI corroboration in grade_reasons. Stage1/2 disagree by 0.258 (F5 gives NYY 73%, full game only 52.6%) — a gap the 6/20 lesson says a lone F-signal cannot rescue. Edge +1.45pp is negligible and CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.533 in the bottom bucket without a CONFIRM anchor. No corroborating dimension to lean on. SKIP confirmed.

CHC @ CIN.reasoning: Negative edge (-2.58pp) and no F-signal fires; only team_quality and Stage-agree contribute pre-cap. Pipeline already SKIPs correctly and CAP 10 confirms at pick_prob=0.533, essentially a coin-flip. Nothing in the postgame archive argues for elevating a bare, signal-thin coin-flip game. SKIP confirmed.

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: Pre-cap score=3 rests on F3=4816.4 and PQI Δ-13.5 only — no SP-xERA dimension in the grade, missing the four-dimension bar the CLE@MIA rescue rule requires. HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.535 without CONFIRM, and edge +1.56pp sits outside the [4,15]pp staking band. The same matchup won unstaked on 7/10 under a similarly thin stack, but one data point is insufficient to elevate conviction here.

SEA @ TB.reasoning: Clean, uncapped A-grade (score=5): F1*=1.14 starred but corroborated by SP edge 1.12 agreeing and PQI +17.2 confirming (satisfies the F1*-corroboration rule), Stage agree at Δ=0.07. Edge +10.07pp sits comfortably inside the healthy [4,15]pp band with no hard cap firing anywhere in grade_reasons — the strongest, most complete signal stack on today's slate. GOLD confirmed at full conviction.

PHI @ DET.reasoning: SP edge 0.49 xERA AGAINST DET plus PQI -14.1 AGAINST — the dual-opposing D-grade pattern that went 0-for-3 directionally on 7/9 (ATL@PIT, KC@NYM, CHC@BAL). Edge +17.16pp sits in the extreme zone just below HARD CAP 6's 25pp threshold, consistent with prior calibrator overstatement. Skubal's track record argues against fully fading DET and no live news confirms a PHI thesis, so no OVERRIDE — just treat the DET direction as unreliable. SKIP confirmed.

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: Pre-cap score=5 with all four dimensions affirming simultaneously: F-signal (F1*=1.04 corroborated by F3=2778.7 and F5=+0.93), SP edge 0.73 agrees, PQI confirms Δ-4.3, Stage agree Δ=0.02. HARD CAP 10 zeroed it at pick_prob=0.532, but this is the exact same matchup and rescue profile validated on 7/10 (CLE won 3-2 after an identical four-dimension rescue). Applying the same rescue rule: restore full PLATINUM.

LAA @ MIN.reasoning: Pre-cap score=3 (F1 agrees, PQI confirms) but Stage1/2 disagree by 0.14 (-1) and HARD CAP 1 fires on the -1.61pp negative edge. CAP 1 is the archive's most validated cap, non-negotiable even on elite stacks (ATL@STL 7/10, BOS@CHW 7/7). confidence_downgrade and bullpen_fatigue flags compound the caution. SKIP confirmed.

OAK @ CHW.reasoning: SP edge 2.00 xERA AGAINST CHW plus PQI -30.9 AGAINST — the largest dual-opposition gap on the slate, matching the same D-grade fade pattern validated repeatedly since 7/8 (OAK@DET, LAA@TEX). Pick_prob is a near coin-flip (0.505). No countervailing information available. SKIP confirmed; treat CHW direction as low-confidence.

ATL @ STL.reasoning: grade_reasons show a clean, uncapped score=5 stack (F1*=1.14 corroborated by F3=1305.6, SP edge 1.04 agrees, PQI +3.2, Stage agree) with edge +11.3pp inside the healthy band and no hard cap listed — yet the pipeline's own tier column already reads SKIP with no visible gate cited (why_skipped just restates 'tier SKIP'). Without visibility into that upstream gate, deferring to the model's own SKIP rather than elevating beyond its stated tier. SKIP confirmed, flagged for transparency.

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: PLATINUM explicitly WITHHELD by the pipeline (SP edge 0.68 xERA against HOU, PQI Δ+5.8 against, Stage1/2 disagree by 0.20 — F5 gives HOU 84% but full game barely 50.1%). Three independent dimensions oppose the pick simultaneously, the same 'direction is noise' configuration flagged in the 7/8 LAA@TEX postgame (five-signal opposition, 13-1 blowout). Pick_prob is a coin-flip (0.5009). SKIP confirmed; HOU direction treated as unreliable.

COL @ SF.reasoning: Pre-cap score=8, the strongest raw stack on the slate (F1*=1.73 corroborated by F3=1372.3 and F5=+2.06, SP edge 1.40 agrees, PQI +17.8, PLATINUM bonus). But HARD CAP 4 (Stage1/2 delta 0.13 combined with confidence_downgrade=True) forces score to 1 — a distinct, non-rescued cap with no documented exception in the archive (unlike CAP10's four-dimension rule). acute_roster and bullpen_fatigue flags add caution. SKIP confirmed; an elite stack alone doesn't override CAP 4.

ARI @ LAD.reasoning: Mitch Bratt has only 54 Statcast pitches season-to-date, below the 100-pitch xERA-scoring minimum. The pipeline correctly withholds a pick until his sample clears. No directional evaluation is possible without a scored SP edge. Hard stop confirmed.

TOR @ SD.reasoning: SP edge 1.75 xERA AGAINST SD plus PQI -27.8 AGAINST — another instance of the dual-opposing D-grade fade pattern (matches OAK@CHW today and the 7/8-7/9 archive). Buehler's injury-related regression likely explains part of the SP gap. acute_roster and bullpen_fatigue flags compound caution. Market prices SD only marginally above coin-flip (fair_prob=0.4575) despite the model's 59.7% claim — a real edge (+13.91pp) built on an unreliable SP dimension. SKIP confirmed.
